plan.reasoning:
This is the first attempt (no history). The adsorbate is O=N (nitroso, SMILES), with atom index 0 = O (SP2, no radical) and index 1 = N (SP2, no radical). Both atoms have lone pairs and can donate to the surface. The molecule is a stable closed-shell species (adsorbate_type: Molecule).

For nitroso (O=N) on transition metal surfaces, N-end adsorption at ontop sites is the most commonly reported and energetically favorable binding mode, as N has a lone pair and is the more reactive end. The surface contains Mo, S, and W atoms. Mo and W are transition metals with d-orbitals that strongly interact with N lone pairs. I will start with N-end (index 1) adsorption at an ontop Mo site, as Mo is a common active site in MoS2-based catalysts and Mo-ontop is a well-defined site available on this surface.

Plan: site_type=ontop, surface_binding_atoms=['Mo'], adsorbate_binding_indices=[1] (N atom binds to Mo ontop).
